title: Full GPTQ + XSA-all + SWA/EMA (val_bpb=1.1158, 3-seed mean=1.1163)
author: Robby955
body:
## Summary

Full GPTQ quantization with XSA on all 11 layers, SWA/EMA weight blending, and comprehensive TTT/quantization analysis. Built on PR #589's architecture (11L GEPA, VE128, Late QAT).

**Best score: val_bpb = 1.1158** (seed 1337, 8×H100 SXM, no TTT)

### 3-Seed Validation

| Seed | Pre-quant | Sliding BPB | Artifact Size |
|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| 1337 | 1.1356 | **1.1158** | 15.92 MB |
| 42 | 1.1367 | 1.1168 | 15.97 MB |
| 2025 | 1.1362 | 1.1164 | 15.95 MB |
| **Mean** | **1.1362** | **1.1163** | |

### Key Contributions

**1. Full GPTQ halves the quantization gap (0.008 → 0.004 BPB)**

Cholesky-based GPTQ with act-order column permutation and block-wise error compensation (block_size=128). Calibrated on 256 training batches with 1% diagonal damping.

| Method | Quant Gap | Artifact Size |
|--------|-----------|---------------|
| Naive int6 (grid search) | +0.0083 BPB | 15.81 MB |
| **Full GPTQ (Cholesky)** | **+0.0039 BPB** | **15.92 MB** |

**2. AdamW TTT catastrophically destroys GPTQ-calibrated weights**

| TTT Config | Post-TTT BPB | TTT Effect |
|------------|-------------|------------|
| No TTT (sliding window) | **1.1158** | baseline |
| AdamW lr=5e-4, 10 epochs | 1.1931 | **+0.077 worse** |
| SGD lr=2e-3, mom=0.9, 3-5 ep | 1.1173 | ~neutral |
| SGD lr=1e-3, 5 ep | 1.1173 | ~neutral |
| SGD lr=5e-4, 10 ep | ~1.117 | ~neutral |
| No EB-TTT, SGD | 1.1172 | ~neutral |

GPTQ places weights using Hessian curvature optimization. AdamW's per-parameter adaptive rates fight the GPTQ structure. **TTT is neutral-to-harmful on GPTQ-quantized models regardless of optimizer, learning rate, or per-layer scaling.**

**3. GPTQ damping factor has negligible impact**

Swept damping factor from 0.003 to 0.02 using REQUANT_ONLY mode (load saved fp32 model, re-quantize, eval). All values give ~1.11626 BPB — within 0.00001. The Cholesky solve is robust to damping choice.

**4. XSA-all improves training quality**

XSA on all 11 layers (vs last 4) improves sliding window by 0.0013. Combined with warmdown 4000 (vs 3500), yields 16 SWA snapshots for smoother averaging.

**5. EB-TTT with Born-rule scaling (SNR²)**

Novel per-layer TTT gradient scaling inspired by quantum probability amplitudes. While mathematically elegant, **EB-TTT provides no measurable BPB improvement on GPTQ-quantized models** — the quantization has already optimized weight placement beyond what TTT can improve.

### Architecture & Training

- **Base**: PR #589 (11L GEPA, 8 GQA heads / 4 KV, VE128, Late QAT)
- **Changed**: XSA all 11 layers, Full GPTQ, LZMA compression (preset=9 extreme), SWA+EMA 50/50 blend, warmdown 4000
- **Training**: ~6750 steps at 88.9ms/step on 8×H100 SXM, 27.1M params
- **Artifact**: 15.92 MB (code: 94KB, compressed weights: 15.82MB)

### Credits

- @RoyiRa for PR #589 base architecture
- @thwu1, @unnir, @JoeProAI for components adopted in #589
- @saml212, @kshitizz36 for GPTQ reference implementations (#609, #626)
- @CiprianFlorin-Ifrim for int5+Soft-Round QAT reference (#606)
